hypothesis: Fine-tuning a domain-specific language model on MatPROV's PROV-DM graph representations will yield higher structural-level F1-scores for synthesis procedure extraction than the best-performing prompted general-purpose model (o4-mini with one-shot prompting, F1 ≈ 0.835), and this performance gain will be disproportionately larger for complex procedures containing branching or converging subgraphs (≥15 nodes) compared to linear procedures (<10 nodes), because exposure to diverse graph topologies during fine-tuning specifically improves the model's ability to resolve non-sequential causal dependencies that prompt engineering alone cannot adequately convey.
